name: How do installers protect my home from weather during the job?

mainEntity.name: What should a homeowner expect for dust control, rain protection, and interior protection during window installation?
mainEntity.text: What should a homeowner expect for dust control, rain protection, and interior protection during window installation?

acceptedAnswer.text: A professional window crew should take steps to protect both the interior and exterior of the home. That usually includes drop cloths, protective covering for floors and furniture, careful removal of old units, and prompt coverage of any opening that is temporarily exposed. In windier coastal areas, weather protection matters even more because a sudden change in conditions can make an opening vulnerable fast. Homeowners should ask how the crew handles rain, where debris will be staged, and whether the installation will be paused if conditions become unsafe. If the job is large, the contractor should explain how many openings will be open at one time and what temporary sealing method will be used. Clean protection procedures are a good indicator of an organized installation team.
acceptedAnswer.description: Installers should use drop cloths, protect floors and furniture, seal off work areas, and cover exposed openings quickly if weather changes. In coastal areas, good crews plan for wind and rain so the house is not left exposed.
